May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny **Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 232 **Prerequisites** Master in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) --- Allies intuitively follow your orders. Choose an ally within 30 feet and then attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against a hard DC for that ally’s level. This counts as a preparatory action to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) that ally. Regardless of your result, the ally is then temporarily immune to your further uses of Seasoned Command for 1 minute. **Special** Once per hour, you can [Take the Lead](/Rules.aspx?ID=3450) of a troop as a free action (rather than 2 actions). Outside of combat, you can establish leadership of a troop and grant a [skirmish bond](/Rules.aspx?ID=3452) in half the normal time (5 minutes and 4 hours, respectively). **Critical Success** You immediately gain an additional reaction that can be used only to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) the chosen ally, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your skill check or attack roll to Aid.
**Success** As critical success, but you don’t gain the circumstance bonus.
**Failure** You don’t gain an additional reaction.
name: Seasoned Command

>
[Tactical Acumen](/Feats.aspx?ID=8984) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 232

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)

</column>

---

Even if you don’t know much about its constituent creatures, you can always recognize a troop’s capabilities through features like the formation they move in or even more subtle hints others might miss. You can always use [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) when you [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) to identify a troop. This works like using an applicable Lore to identify a creature. If you’re legendary in Warfare Lore, reduce any adjustment to the Recall Knowledge check’s DC caused by the troop’s rarity by 5.

If you [lead a troop](/Rules.aspx?ID=3439), when you successfully use Warfare Lore to identify a troop, that troop takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against your troop’s offensive abilities until the end of your next turn.
name: Tactical Acumen

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 18 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Add the [Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) trait to Swimming Charge._ **Requirements** You are swimming in water. --- You swim directly into a foe, attempting to push them out of the way. [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) up to your Speed, attempting to enter the space of a foe that’s your size or smaller. Roll an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check and compare the result of the Fortitude DC of the target creature. If the foe can’t move into an empty space (if it is surrounded by solid objects or other creatures, for example), you automatically critically fail this check. **Critical Success** The target takes bludgeoning damage equal to your level plus twice your Strength modifier and must move into the empty space directly behind it; you move into the space it vacated. The target must move even if doing so places it in harm’s way.
**Success** As critical success, but the damage is equal to your level plus your Strength modifier.
**Failure** The target takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier and you end your movement before entering its space.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected and you end your movement before entering its space.
name: Swimming Charge

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 18 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Can be used in less than 20 feet of water, ending its effect at the water line._ **Requirements** You are swimming in water. --- With a flick of your tail, you direct the water around you to move creatures. You move water in a 20-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388); this line must be entirely in water. Each creature in this line must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. A creature can choose to fail the save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) or pulled 5 feet in the direction of your choice. All creatures must be moved in the same direction.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is pushed or pulled 10 feet.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature is pushed or pulled 10 feet, and the creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn.
name: Coerce the Current

You reach out and grab an adjacent creature (on nearby land or in the water) and pull it below the surface. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the creature. On a success, if the creature is on land, in addition to the normal effects of Grapple, you pull the creature into the water in a space adjacent to you. If the creature is already in the water, on a success, in addition the normal effects of Grapple, you drag the creature 10 feet deeper into the water, moving 10 feet with the creature. Moving a creature into water or deeper into water using Steal for the Depths is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) for the creature but not for you.
name: Steal for the Depths

You’ve learned to use your own athletic abilities and springy legs to help others who need a boost with their leaps. The triggering ally gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). This bonus increases to +2 if you’re an expert in Athletics, +3 if you’re a master, and +4 if you’re legendary. Additionally, the triggering ally’s [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) distance on a successful check increases by 2 feet for a High Jump or 5 feet for a Long Jump, even if it would allow the ally to Leap further than their land Speed.

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 21 **Frequency** once per day --- You have a connection to water and are able to call upon it to batter your foes and carry them away. You create a massive wave of water that crashes in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet that moves in the direction of your choice. All creatures in the area take 14d6 bludgeoning damage and could be swept away. At the beginning of subsequent turns for 3 rounds, the wave moves 30 feet in the direction you chose; creatures in the area who aren’t already swept away by the wave take the bludgeoning damage. Creatures being swept away are trapped within the wave and can attempt to Escape against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, to end the effect. Otherwise, the creature remains swept away until the wave stops moving. The wave also extinguishes all non-magical fires in its path automatically. Creatures are affected based on the result of their Reflex saves with a DC equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) 10 feet in the direction of the wave, but not swept away.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is fully swept away by the wave. The creature moves along with the wave until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296).
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature takes double damage and must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) (unless it can breathe in water).
name: Frog Tide

You developed techniques for discovering a target’s weaknesses to poisons using your forensic knowledge. When you study a location that a creature spends a lot of time in, such as a bedroom or office, you learn medical conditions, reactions, or sensitivities that make them susceptible to poisons. Subsequently, whenever you craft an [alchemical poison](/Rules.aspx?ID=3185), you can tailor it to a creature you have studied in this way, giving that creature a –2 circumstance penalty to Fortitude saves against the poison and giving other creatures a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against it.

Spend enough time in Hell Harbor, and you pick up the paranoia encouraged by Arronax Endymion. Maybe you served under him on the _Tyrannous_, escaped his executioners, or had a friend who wasn’t so lucky; whatever the reason, you have some of his wariness. Unless you’re a willing target, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saves against creatures you consider an ally and creatures who are [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) by you. </column>

---

An animal has become your loyal ally, perhaps after you liberated it from another ship or found it on a remote island. You gain the [Pet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5186) general feat. Your pet gains an additional pet Shackles Options ability, which you can choose from the standard pet abilities or the following familiar abilities: [accompanist](/Familiars.aspx?ID=108), [damage avoidance](/Familiars.aspx?ID=110), [independent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=115), [partner in crime](/Familiars.aspx?ID=119), [speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=124), and [valet](/Familiars.aspx?ID=128). 

In addition, you learn a common 1st- or 2nd-rank spell that targets your familiar, and can cast it once per day as a [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232). You can instead learn [_phase familiar_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1884) and can cast it once per hour as a primal innate spell; it’s not a focus spell for you. When you cast the chosen spell, you target your pirate’s pet instead of a familiar.
name: Pirate's Pet

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 93 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) **Access** You're from the Shackles **Requirements** You are above an enemy. --- You’ve learned how to make a quick descent with a sudden stop—directly on an enemy. You intentionally fall onto an enemy within 10 feet of you horizontally and can accurately target them from up to a height equal to 5 feet times your level. You take damage as [normal](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352); the enemy must attempt a Reflex save against your class DC with the following results, instead of the usual rules for [falling on a creature](/Rules.aspx?ID=2353). If an ability reduces the amount of damage you take from falling, the enemy’s damage is based on the damage you would have taken before that reduction. In addition, if you have a means of controlling your descent, such as holding onto a rope or sliding down an adjacent wall, you do not land [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) even if you take damage from the fall. **Critical Success** The enemy takes no damage.
**Success** The enemy takes bludgeoning damage equal to one quarter the falling damage you took.
**Failure** The creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to half the falling damage you took and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage you took from the fall, is knocked prone, and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
name: Cannonball Fall

<title

>
[Marine Marauder Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=9030) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 106

**Archetype** [Marine Marauder](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=385)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)

</column>

---

You’re as skilled in combat underwater as on land. You gain the [Underwater Marauder](/Feats.aspx?ID=5230) skill feat, and when you roll a success on an Athletics check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), you get a critical success instead. In addition, checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) nets you use are attempted against your class DC instead of the listed DC, and you can keep hold of a target you’ve grappled with a net with one hand.

<title level="2">Marine Marauder Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aquatic Passage](/Feats.aspx?ID=9036), [Aquatic Pirouette](/Feats.aspx?ID=9031), [Concealing Currents](/Feats.aspx?ID=9037), [Like a Fish in Water](/Feats.aspx?ID=9032), [Marine Combat Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=9033), [Marine Jet](/Feats.aspx?ID=9041), [Pressure Change](/Feats.aspx?ID=9039), [Reactive Tangle](/Feats.aspx?ID=9034), [Reel 'Em In](/Feats.aspx?ID=9042), [Shark Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=9038), [Stay Here!](/Feats.aspx?ID=9040), [Surface Rush](/Feats.aspx?ID=9035), [Take in the Catch](/Feats.aspx?ID=9043)
name: Marine Marauder Dedication

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 107 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Unclear if this ability frees a Grabbed creature._ **Archetype** [Marine Marauder](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=385) **Prerequisites** [Marine Marauder Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=9030) **Requirements** You have a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351), are underwater, and have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). --- You sink or rise rapidly alongside your target, altering their internal pressure. **Failure** The target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** The target is sickened 2.
